title: HTTP Representations, Web Standards, and Node Streams
description: A Node 24 and TypeScript 7 team receives a changed file-delivery case: A download endpoint guesses MIME types, buffers large bodies, ignores validators and Range, and treats Content-Disposition text as trusted HTML. The existing implementation trusts one compile-time or promise-level claim that runtime. Learners cumulatively produce TypeScript representation and stream contract through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, define media type, content length, content encoding, language, disposition, filename parameters, and cache metadata as representation facts.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, apply ETag and Last-Modified validators with If-Match, If-None-Match, If-Range, 304, 412, and changed-state evidence.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, parse and evaluate single byte ranges, suffix ranges, satisfiable bounds, 206, Content-Range, Accept-Ranges, and 416.
